data.user_prompt: Where can someone get mental health case management in Louisville, KY?
data.system_message: Answer as a local-search assistant for a person in Louisville, Kentucky. Prefer current local providers and cite source URLs when available.
data.tag: lmhg llm response perplexity Where can someone get mental health case management in Louisville, KY?

sections.text:
Someone can get mental health case management in Louisville, KY through several local providers, including **Louisville Mental Health Group**, **Best Life Mental Health Services**, **Wellspring**, **Autism Behavior Support Services**, and **Seven Counties Services**[1][2][3][4][5].

Here are the key options with details on eligibility and contact methods:

| Provider | Service Type | Eligibility | Contact Info |
|----------|--------------|-------------|--------------|
| **Louisville Mental Health Group** | General Case Management | Open to all; assists with housing, employment, medical care, transportation[1] | Visit [louisvillementalhealth.org/case-management/](https://louisvillementalhealth.org/case-management/) |
| **Best Life Mental Health Services** | Targeted Case Management | **Medicaid members** with a qualified mental health diagnosis (social/emotional disorders)[2] | Call **(502) 333-9466** or visit [bestlifelouisville.com/case-management/](https://www.bestlifelouisville.com/case-management/) |
| **Wellspring** | Case Management for Mentally Ill Patients | Adults with **serious mental illness**[3] | Visit [wellspringky.org/case-management/](https://wellspringky.org/case-management/) |
| **Autism Behavior Support Services** | Targeted Case Management | **Medicaid-funded**; for children with severe emotional disabilities, adults with serious mental illness, or substance use disorders[4] | Visit [cultivatebhe.com/targeted-case-management/](https://cultivatebhe.com/targeted-case-management/) |
| **Seven Counties Services** | General Case Management | **Everyone** regardless of diagnosis or insurance status[5] | Call **(502) 589-1100** or request an appointment online at [sevencounties.org](https://sevencounties.org) |

**Additional Notes:**
- If you or someone you know is in crisis, the **988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline** is available 24/7[5].
- For longer-term treatment needs, providers like **Norton Healthcare** can connect you to community-based long-term behavioral health providers[6].
- The **Kentucky Department for Behavioral Health, Developmental and Intellectual Disabilities** administers state and federally funded programs for mental health and substance use[8].

For immediate assistance, **Seven Counties Services** is a strong starting point as they serve all individuals regardless of insurance[5]. If you have Medicaid, **Best Life** or **Autism Behavior Support Services** may offer specialized targeted case management[2][4].
